interface: Cloud Client Library for Python (google-cloud-pubsub 2.39.0), streaming pull subscriber surface

fields_and_defaults.max_bytes: 104857600 — max total size of received-but-unprocessed messages before the stream pauses
fields_and_defaults.max_messages: 1000 — max count of received-but-unprocessed messages before the stream pauses
fields_and_defaults.max_lease_duration: 3600 — max seconds to hold a lease before dropping the message from lease management
fields_and_defaults.min_duration_per_lease_extension: 0 (ignored by default); when set must be between 10 and 600 inclusive
fields_and_defaults.max_duration_per_lease_extension: 0 (ignored by default); when set must be between 10 and 600 inclusive; bounds redelivery delay if the subscriber dies

subscribe.signature: SubscriberClient.subscribe(subscription, callback, flow_control=(), scheduler=None, use_legacy_flow_control=False, await_callbacks_on_shutdown=False) -> StreamingPullFuture
subscribe.callback: called with one google.cloud.pubsub_v1.subscriber.message.Message per delivery; exceptions must not escape the callback

message_surface.properties: data (bytes), attributes (str->str map), message_id, ack_id, ordering_key, size, publish_time, delivery_attempt (None unless the subscription has a dead-letter policy)
message_surface.ack: Message.ack() — best-effort unless exactly-once delivery is enabled, so processing must be idempotent; ack only after the work is actually done
message_surface.nack: Message.nack() — causes redelivery, immediately or delayed, to this or another subscriber; the failure path for messages the handler could not process
message_surface.modify_ack_deadline: Message.modify_ack_deadline(seconds) — seconds must be between 0 and 600; values below 10 are advised against by the library docs, and 0 makes the message immediately eligible for redelivery (a nack in effect)
message_surface.dispatch: each of these enqueues a request (AckRequest / NackRequest / ModAckRequest) onto the library's internal request queue — the injectable surface the tests observe

shutdown.clean_stop: StreamingPullFuture.cancel() first, then StreamingPullFuture.result() to block until the shutdown completes — in exactly that order
shutdown.await_callbacks_on_shutdown: subscribe() flag controlling whether in-flight callbacks are awaited during shutdown; must be forwarded verbatim

policy.lease_extension_bounds: pubstream.LeaseHandle.extend accepts only integers in [10, 600]: 600 is the documented modifyAckDeadline maximum, and sub-10 extensions (including the 0-second nack) are rejected as extension values
policy.ack_after_completion: a message is acked only after its handler returns; a raising handler produces exactly one nack and no ack

test_fixture_notes.note: tests build real Message objects over a local queue.Queue and a fake SubscriberClient/StreamingPullFuture pair; no emulator, no network
